primary_factors: No snow/ice accumulation signal (0" forecast; precip type mainly rain/drizzle) and temps hover just above freezing then rise—Michigan-standard threshold for closure not met
Primary hazard is dense fog/near-zero visibility roughly 1 AM–9 AM, including the 6–8 AM bus window; this is a delay/operational-risk scenario more than a full closure scenario
Ice risk appears patchy/localized (bridges/overpasses/shaded rural spots) rather than widespread—important for bus caution but usually not enough alone for district-wide closure
No confirmed neighboring-district closures/delays and no Rockford-specific announcement/buzz as of the latest sweep; weak “domino effect” pressure
Power outage levels are low/stable in Kent County (dozens of customers), with no evidence of a widespread outage event affecting schools
decision_rationale: All experts converge on ‘not a snow event’ with mild wind chills and clear roads from a snow-removal perspective. The only credible disruption driver is unusually persistent dense fog with potentially very low visibility during the bus window, plus localized slickness risk on bridges if surface temps briefly touch freezing. In Michigan (and specifically West Michigan), fog alone more commonly produces an OPEN day or a DELAY rather than a full closure unless visibility is both widespread and stays near-zero through bus launch. News/community signals (no confirmed neighboring closures, no Rockford announcement, no clear crash cluster tied to Fri morning fog) support staying on the low end for a full closure. Given the safety analyst’s updated view that fog could make the morning commute ‘significant’ and that cancellation becomes plausible only if near-zero visibility is widespread and persistent, the best Brier-optimized call is: low probability of a full snow day closure, but meaningful probability of a delay. I’m setting 10% for full closure to account for the scenario where the fog verifies worse than modeled (or persists longer) across rural routes district-wide.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 5.0% spread. Average probability: 9%.
